_instructions: Add your own tracks and goals here. The site renders these alongside the base roadmap. Each track needs an id, title, description, color, and an array of nodes. Each node needs an id, title, and description. Resources and tryIt are optional. Say 'Add a goal to my roadmap' to your AI tool (or run /add-goal in Claude Code).

tracks.title: Make It Mine
tracks.description: Customize your OS for how you work

nodes.title: Personalize agents.md
nodes.description: Edit agents.md to change how AI behaves in your repo. Add rules specific to your work style, your industry, or your preferences.
nodes.tryIt: Open agents.md and add a line under Behavior. Example: 'When I ask about pricing, reference my operating manual and suggest options based on my revenue model.'

nodes.title: Create a custom command
nodes.description: Commands are markdown files in .claude/commands/. Write instructions, save as .md, and it becomes a slash command. No code needed.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Create a /weekly command that reviews my journal entries from this week, checks my open issues, and suggests what to focus on next week.'

nodes.title: Create a custom skill
nodes.description: Skills teach AI deeper capabilities. Create a SKILL.md file with instructions for something you want AI to consistently do well, like reviewing content in your brand voice or generating client proposals.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Create a skill that reviews my writing and checks it matches [describe your style]. Save it to .claude/skills/my-voice/SKILL.md.'

nodes.title: Install a community skill
nodes.description: Browse skills built by others at skills.sh. Install with one command. They drop into your .claude/skills/ folder and AI picks them up automatically.
nodes.tryIt: Run: npx skills find [something you need help with]

tracks.title: Go Multiplayer
tracks.description: Work with others through your OS

nodes.title: Use branches for experiments
nodes.description: A branch lets you try things without affecting the main version. If the experiment works, merge it. If not, delete the branch.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Create a branch called experiment, make a change to my operating manual, then walk me through how to merge it back.'

nodes.title: Review changes with Pull Requests
nodes.description: PRs are how changes get reviewed before merging. They show exactly what changed, line by line. For teams, they prevent the 'someone overwrote my changes' problem that plagues Google Drive.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Walk me through creating a pull request for my experiment branch. Explain what I'm seeing in the diff view.'

nodes.title: Invite a collaborator
nodes.description: Add someone to your repo. They can create issues, submit PRs, and use the same commands and skills. Your personal OS becomes a team OS.
nodes.tryIt: Go to Settings > Collaborators and invite someone you work with.

nodes.title: Deploy your roadmap site
nodes.description: Your repo includes a roadmap site and a GitHub Action that auto-deploys it. Enable GitHub Pages and your progress dashboard goes live.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Help me enable GitHub Pages for this repo and deploy the roadmap site.'

nodes.title: Start a project in apps/
nodes.description: The apps/ folder is where your projects live. Each gets its own folder. Could be a landing page, a tool, a client site, or anything you build.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Create a new project in apps/ for [describe your project]. Set up a README and help me plan the first version.'

nodes.title: Add a custom domain
nodes.description: Point your own domain at your GitHub Pages site instead of using the default yourname.github.io URL.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'Walk me through setting up a custom domain for my GitHub Pages site.'

nodes.title: Set up an automation
nodes.description: GitHub Actions run automatically when things happen in your repo. Your repo already has one for deploying the roadmap site. Add more for whatever you need.
nodes.tryIt: Ask your AI tool: 'What GitHub Actions would be useful for my business? Look at my operating manual for context and suggest 2-3 automations.'
